title: A Girl Power First at the Kentucky Derby!
summary: History galloped into Churchill Downs on Saturday! A horse named Golden Tempo won the 152nd Kentucky Derby, and his trainer, Cherie DeVaux, became the first woman ever to train the winner of this famous race. Can you believe it took 152 years? Golden Tempo wasn't the favorite — his odds were 23-1, which means most people didn't think he'd win. But with jockey Jose Ortiz riding, he zoomed from the back of the pack and charged down the stretch to win. "I don't even have any words right now," DeVaux said, super happy. "Jose did a masterful job." DeVaux is only the second woman to train any Triple Crown race winner, after Jena Antonucci won the Belmont Stakes in 2023. This was DeVaux's very first time having a horse in the Derby, and she won! Earlier in the week, she realized how important it is to be a role model for girls who might want to work with horses someday. She's just the 18th woman to ever have a horse in the Kentucky Derby. Jose Ortiz had a great weekend — he also won the Kentucky Oaks race for fillies (girl horses) the day before. The Derby had only 18 horses instead of 20 because one horse, Great White, got scared and flipped over, so vets said he couldn't run. Another horse, Silent Tactic, had a hurt foot and dropped out earlier.
why_it_matters: This win teaches us that big dreams can come true even if you are the first person like you to try, and it can encourage other kids to follow unusual paths.

keywords.explanation: A number showing how likely a horse is to win a race; higher numbers mean fewer people expect it to win.
A person who rides a horse in a race.
A series of three famous and difficult horse races in the United States.
A term for young female horses.
A word used in the name of important horse races, like the Belmont Stakes.
A person others look up to and want to be like.

questions.question: Who trained the horse that won the Kentucky Derby?
What were the winning horse's odds?
Who rode Golden Tempo during the race?
How many years did it take for a woman to train the Derby winner?
Why didn't Great White run in the Derby?
Which race did Jose Ortiz win the day before the Derby?

background_read: The Kentucky Derby is called 'the most exciting two minutes in sports' and is held every year at Churchill Downs in Louisville, Kentucky.
Horse racing has a long history, and the Triple Crown (Derby, Preakness, and Belmont) is one of the hardest achievements in the sport.
For many years, horse training and racing were mostly done by men, but women have been slowly breaking through as jockeys and trainers.
Article_Structure: WHO: Trainer Cherie DeVaux, jockey Jose Ortiz, and the horse Golden Tempo
WHAT: Golden Tempo won the Kentucky Derby as a long-shot, making Cherie DeVaux the first woman trainer to win.
WHERE: At Churchill Downs racetrack during the 152nd Kentucky Derby.
WHY: Her victory is a historic first that shows girls they can succeed in sports where women were once rare.

perspectives.description: She feels overjoyed and hopes her victory will inspire young girls to work with horses and believe in themselves.
Many fans are excited to see a historic first and love the thrill of an unexpected winner crossing the finish line.
Some people may have thought a woman would never train a Derby winner, but now this achievement is changing old ideas about who can succeed.
